title: Peanut Butter Banana Toast

subs.name: almond butter
subs.notes: lower allergen option; slightly milder flavour

subs.name: a drizzle of jaggery syrup (melt jaggery in warm water)

steps.text: Toast the bread slices to your preferred doneness — light golden for a soft result, dark golden for a crunch that holds up under the topping.
While the toast is still hot, spread a generous tablespoon of peanut butter on each slice. The warmth will help it spread smoothly to the edges.
Slice the banana into 5 mm rounds and lay them overlapping on top of the peanut butter.
Drizzle honey over both slices and add a pinch of cinnamon if using. Serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: peanut → almond butter (lower allergen option; slightly milder flavour); honey → a drizzle of jaggery syrup (melt jaggery in warm water) (a more Indian-pantry friendly sweetener); banana → sliced apple or mango (different fruit, same concept)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Toast bread on a dry tawa instead of a toaster — press lightly and flip once for even golden colour. Indian peanut butter (Sundrop, Dr. Oetker) is widely available; alternatively, blend roasted peanuts in a mixie with a pinch of salt for a homemade version.
